---
title: "Ad Targeting A/B Testing Analysis Outputs"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)

# Load data
data <- read_excel("yelp.xlsx", sheet = "yelp_data")
```

## Revenue Trend

```{r revenue-trend, fig.width=8, fig.height=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
daily_revenue <- data %>%
  group_by(Date, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(daily_revenue = sum(Revenue), .groups = "drop")

p_revenue <- ggplot(daily_revenue, aes(x = Date, y = daily_revenue, color = Treatment)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("control" = "#E9A8C6", "treatment" = "#C9B6E4")) +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Revenue Trend by Treatment Group",
    x = "Date",
    y = "Revenue",
    color = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_revenue
ggsave("revenue_trend.png", plot = p_revenue, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

## Metrics Comparison

```{r metrics-comparison, fig.width=8, fig.height=5, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
metrics <- tibble(
  Metric = rep(c("CTR", "STR", "Conversion Rate"), each = 2),
  Treatment = rep(c("Control", "Treatment"), times = 3),
  Value = c(
    0.680, 0.603,   # CTR
    0.943, 0.740,   # STR
    0.759, 0.857    # Conversion Rate
  )
)

p_metrics <- ggplot(metrics, aes(x = Metric, y = Value, fill = Treatment, group = Treatment)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.72), width = 0.64) +
  geom_line(
    aes(
      x = c(1 - 0.18, 2 - 0.18, 3 - 0.18)[match(Metric, c("CTR", "STR", "Conversion Rate"))]
    ),
    data = metrics %>% filter(Treatment == "Control"),
    color = "#E9A8C6",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = c(1 - 0.18, 2 - 0.18, 3 - 0.18)[match(Metric, c("CTR", "STR", "Conversion Rate"))]
    ),
    data = metrics %>% filter(Treatment == "Control"),
    color = "#E9A8C6",
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  geom_line(
    aes(
      x = c(1 + 0.18, 2 + 0.18, 3 + 0.18)[match(Metric, c("CTR", "STR", "Conversion Rate"))]
    ),
    data = metrics %>% filter(Treatment == "Treatment"),
    color = "#C9B6E4",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = c(1 + 0.18, 2 + 0.18, 3 + 0.18)[match(Metric, c("CTR", "STR", "Conversion Rate"))]
    ),
    data = metrics %>% filter(Treatment == "Treatment"),
    color = "#C9B6E4",
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = percent(Value, accuracy = 0.1)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.72),
    vjust = -0.4,
    size = 3.8
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Control" = "#E9A8C6", "Treatment" = "#C9B6E4")) +
  labs(
    title = "Control vs Treatment Across Key Metrics",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

p_metrics
ggsave("metrics_comparison.png", plot = p_metrics, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

## Revenue Significance Test

```{r revenue-t-test, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
t_test_revenue <- t.test(Revenue ~ Treatment, data = data)
t_test_revenue
```

## Power Analysis for CTR

```{r ctr-power-analysis, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(pwr)

ctr_control <- 0.680
ctr_treatment <- 0.603
sample_size <- 87365

effect_size_ctr <- ES.h(p1 = ctr_control, p2 = ctr_treatment)
power_ctr <- pwr.2p.test(h = effect_size_ctr, n = sample_size, sig.level = 0.05)

effect_size_ctr
power_ctr
```
